items.title: Today is your last chance at the 2026 Mario Day Switch game deals
items.summary:
Mario Day 2026 was an eventful one this year and today is your last shot at the official Switch game deals. While many of the physical game deals are long gone, there are still loads of notable digital titles live at Amazon, Best Buy, the eShop, and elsewhere through today only. From the final Super Mario Galaxy Movie trailer and the release of Mario Clash, Mario vs. Donkey Kong, and Mario Tennis classics, to the reveal of upcoming Super Mario minifigure scale LEGO sets and the Mario Kart Luigi build, we also got an official release date for Yoshi and the Mysterious Book and a giant lineup of Switch game deals too. Today, however, is your last chance at the a latter as the last of the Mario Switch game deals are scheduled to be gone for another year at midnight PT tonight. Check them out below.

items.title: A survey of 1,000 hiring managers: 59% say they stress AI's role in layoffs or hiring freezes "because it plays better"; only 9% say AI has fully replaced roles (Gautam Mukunda/Bloomberg)
items.summary:
Gautam Mukunda / Bloomberg:
A survey of 1,000 hiring managers: 59% say they stress AI's role in layoffs or hiring freezes “because it plays better”; only 9% say AI has fully replaced roles  —  Whatever you think about whether artificial intelligence is coming for your job, it has already mastered one corporate skill: hogging the credit.

items.title: Electrified Weekly – EcoFlow DELTA Pro Ultra X + TRAIL 200 DC power stations $1,439+ off, Bluetti Elite 300 launch, Segway and Navee e-scooters, more
items.summary:
Welcome to 9to5Toys Electrified Weekly – the best place to find all of this week’s best deals and new releases to electrify your life and protect Mother Nature. This week’s edition has a large collection of power station and tool deals – many of the former being flash and/or early-bird sales that will be ending with the weekend. Some standouts include Anker’s SOLIX Spring Sale with up to 65% discounts + exclusive bonus savings for our readers – all starting from $120. Speaking of exclusive savings, we also have the bundle of EcoFlow’s 12,288Wh DELTA Pro Ultra X and 192Wh TRAIL 200 DC power stations with $1,439+ savings, the launch of Bluetti’s Elite 300 power station (and bundles) starting from $1,011, and more. We also got some notable EV deals, like Segway’s Max G30P electric scooter at a $550 yearly low until tonight at midnight, as well as the first-ever discounts on the UT5 Ultra X (43 MPH for up to 75 miles) + UT5 Max (31 MPH for up to 62 miles) superscooters, and more.

You’ll find all of these offers ready and waiting for you right here, but head below for some of our favorite picks from the sale.

items.title: Lectric’s one-day Friday the 13th Blackout flash sale offers select increased e-bike savings + bonus mystery gift from $899
items.summary:
Running as part of the ongoing St. Paddy’s Day Sale, Lectric has launched a one-day-only Friday the 13th Blackout Flash Sale that is offering increased savings on black/grey e-bikes + giving you a FREE mystery gift that is revealed at checkout. One solid option is the XPress 750 High-Step Long-Range Commuter e-bike with $444 in FREE bundled gear at $1,299 shipped (plus the extra mystery gift). This bundle would normally run you $1,743 in total, including the $44 value of the mystery gift. This gives you a step-up from the previous $400 bundle that it’ll revert to after this flash sale ends, making it a much more opportune time to grab yours today. Head below to learn all about this e-bike that began my journey with Lectric, as well as browse the full lineup of offers that are only lasting until midnight, broken down into standard-range and long-range sections.
